observed_strengths: PromptVault can sort bounded previews by weakest prompt quality.
CLI scan JSON stays conservative by omitting prompt bodies by default.
The scan result already contains bounded PromptRecord previews internally.
observed_weaknesses: The CLI could report which preview sort was used, but agents still could not inspect the selected weak prompts from stdout.
The documentation said preview-limit could support bounded JSON preview, but the CLI summary did not expose prompt records.
Printing prompt bodies by default would be unsafe, so the missing capability needed an explicit opt-in and a hard cap.

selected_candidate.name: explicit bounded stdout prompt preview
selected_candidate.reason: Let agents inspect a small weak-first repair queue without writing a large Markdown export or dumping prompt bodies by default.
selected_candidate.success_metric: A scan with --weakest-first --include-prompts --preview-limit 5 returns prompt_stdout_count=5 and the first prompt quality is weak while Markdown export remains disabled.

safety_controls: Prompt bodies remain omitted unless --include-prompts is passed.
Stdout prompt preview is capped at 25 records.
The no-export path can be combined with prompt preview to avoid creating a Markdown file.
